mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, triggering negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.5pp within 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail auto sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving sustained corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in GDPC1 and RSAFS yield negative 60-day forward returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 98.0 indicates severe supply-side contraction that markets overprice, creating a short-term mean-reversion bounce in equity prices.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain negative in >70% of instances where INDPRO drops below 98.0.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price indices combined with low nominal policy rates compress real yields and trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, depressing equity valuations.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0 and FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which historically precedes positive equity market momentum over monthly horizons.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between HOUST levels and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 across all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive months of nonfarm payrolls growth below 100,000 signal labor market deceleration that reduces aggregate consumption and triggers negative quarterly equity returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < 100.0 for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show positive mean or t-statistic > 0 when PAYEMS growth remains below 100k for two months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in unemployment confirms immediate economic deterioration, forcing rapid portfolio deleveraging and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of instances where INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises concurrently.
